chunks.text: Order Sheet IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH, CIRCUIT COURT, HYDERABAD CP No. D- 2153 of 2013 [ Sajid Hussain v. Province of Sindh & others] Before: Mr. Justice Adnan Iqbal Chaudhry Mr. Justice Riazat Ali Sahar Petitioner present in person Mr. Rafiq Ahmed Dahri, Asstt: A.G. Date of Hearing & Order: 02.04.2026 O R D E R ADNAN IQBAL CHAUDHRY, J. - For reasons mentioned in the restoration application [MA No. 2975/25], same is allowed and the petition is restored. It is taken up for hearing. 2. The Petitioner was interviewed by the Sindh Public Service Commission [SPSC] for the post of Assistant Sub-Inspector in the Sindh Police Department; however, the SPSC did not recommend him for appointment. The Petitioner prays that the SPSC be directed to conduct a fresh interview. 3. The Petitioner does not cite any provision of law that was violated by SPSC during the interview process, nor the provision of law under which the SPSC can conduct a fresh interview of a candidate once failed. In view of the case of Waheed Gul Khan v. Province of Sindh (2024 SCMR 1701), it is settled that an interview is inherently a subjective evaluation, and unless malafides or bias are demonstrated, the Court does have jurisdiction to substitute the opinion of the interview board. It was further observed by the Supreme Court in that case, that success in the written test is not a measure of personality traits of the candidate such as communication skills, leadership qualities, decision making abilities etc., which can best be gauged at an interview. 4. The Petitioner is unable to show that the Interview Committee of the SPSC harbored any ill will against the Petitioner.

It was further observed by the Supreme Court in that case, that success in the written test is not a measure of personality traits of the candidate such as communication skills, leadership qualities, decision making abilities etc., which can best be gauged at an interview. 4. The Petitioner is unable to show that the Interview Committee of the SPSC harbored any ill will against the Petitioner. Resultantly, the petition is dismissed. JUDGE JUDGE karar_hussain/PS*
